error.signature: Deregistered ECS task definition but old tasks are still running with old image

verdict.summary: Deregistering an ECS task definition does NOT stop running tasks using it. It only prevents new tasks from using that revision. You must force a new deployment to replace running tasks.

dead_ends.action: Deregister old task definition expecting running tasks to stop
dead_ends.why_fails: Deregistering only marks the revision as INACTIVE. Running tasks continue indefinitely with the old config.

dead_ends.action: Update the task definition and wait for ECS to auto-deploy
dead_ends.why_fails: ECS services don't auto-deploy new task definitions. You must explicitly update the service or force a new deployment.

workarounds.action: Force new deployment after updating task definition
Update the service to use the new task definition revision
workarounds.how: aws ecs update-service --cluster my-cluster --service my-service --task-definition my-task:NEW_REVISION

workarounds.action: Use deployment circuit breaker to auto-rollback failed deployments
